description: Webhook events and verification for Standard Checkout

webhook_setup.sandbox_note: Webhooks work in both sandbox and production environments

CHECKOUT.ORDER.APPROVED.description: Buyer approved the order in PayPal
CHECKOUT.ORDER.APPROVED.trigger: After buyer clicks Pay/Approve in PayPal UI

PAYMENT.CAPTURE.PENDING.action: Wait for completion webhook, do not fulfill yet

PAYMENT.CAPTURE.REFUNDED.action: Update order status, process refund in your system

PAYMENT.CAPTURE.REVERSED.trigger: When a dispute is decided against merchant

PAYMENT.AUTHORIZATION.CREATED.action: Record authorization ID for later capture

verification_payload.webhook_id: Your webhook ID from PayPal dashboard

best_practices: ALWAYS verify webhook signatures before processing
Respond with 200 status within 30 seconds to acknowledge receipt
Implement idempotent webhook handlers (webhooks may be delivered more than once)
Use webhook_id from your PayPal dashboard for verification
Store webhook event IDs to detect and handle duplicates
Process webhooks asynchronously — do not block the response
Log all webhook events with event_type and resource.id for debugging
Handle missing webhooks — poll order status as backup
Do not rely solely on webhooks for critical business logic — verify order status via API
security: ALWAYS verify webhook signatures — never trust unverified webhooks
Validate the webhook event type matches expected events
Verify the resource ID matches an order in your system
Use HTTPS for your webhook listener endpoint
Implement rate limiting on your webhook endpoint
